scenario: Aurora Bank is prototyping a Camunda 8.8 onboarding process and runs 2500 instances per day through the draft model. Several activities have no automation yet, so the modeler keeps the default task type that Web Modeler inserts when adding tasks and plans to replace those activities with concrete implementations later.
question: How does the Camunda 8.8 engine treat an undefined task encountered during execution of the 2500 daily instances?

options.text: The engine pauses each instance until a worker claims a job of type io.camunda.undefined and completes the activity explicitly
The engine waits for an operator to manually complete the undefined task in Operate before the process instance may continue
The engine handles the undefined task as a pass-through activity and continues the process instance automatically on arrival
The engine raises an incident on the undefined task until a concrete task implementation is configured in the process model

a.text: Incorrect. Undefined tasks do not produce a job for any worker type; only typed task variants such as service tasks emit jobs the engine waits on for completion.

b.text: Incorrect. The documentation states the engine continues automatically on arrival; nothing is queued for an Operate user to complete by hand at the undefined task.

c.text: Correct. For the engine, an undefined task is handled as a pass-through activity that automatically continues the process instance at the moment it arrives, with no worker or operator interaction.

d.text: Incorrect. Undefined tasks are an intentional modeling state used while work is unspecified or unautomated and do not cause an incident; the engine simply passes through them.

explanation: An undefined task models work outside the engine or work not yet automated; the engine treats it as a pass-through activity that continues the process instance automatically when reached.
